page: OVHcloud c2-120 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 32 dedicated physical Intel cores running at 3.0 GHz, with one thread per core and no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 120.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided by a 400 GB SSD, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the c2-120 in the average tier for most operations, with a single-core Geekbench score of 1226.0 and a multi-core score of 11504.0. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a notable strength, reaching 253.18 GB/sec, which falls into the top 25% of tested instances. However, the system exhibits limitations in LLM text generation, scoring 37.21 tokens/sec on a small 135M model and 6.29 tokens/sec on a medium 7B model, reflecting its lack of GPU acceleration.

Given its dedicated 32-core Intel architecture and lack of hyperthreading, the instance offers predictable compute density for parallel processing tasks. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU execution without the overhead of shared threads. This makes the c2-120 suitable for multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose compute workloads that do not require specialized matrix or vector extensions beyond standard AVX.
description: OVHcloud c2-120 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical Intel cores on an x86_64 architecture with a 3.0 GHz clock speed. Running on the KVM hypervisor, it provides 120.0 GB of RAM (3.75 GB per core), a 400 GB local SSD, and a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show average performance across most metrics, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 11504.0 and a strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 253.18 GB/sec. However, LLM text generation performance is weak due to the absence of hardware accelerators. This instance is best suited for parallel CPU tasks, software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded data decompression workloads.
